title: crocus biflorus subsp. tauri
intro: Crocus biflorus subsp. tauri, also known as the Eastern or Turkish Crocus, is a subspecies of the crocus flower, native to parts of Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Crocus biflorus subsp. tauri include relief from pain, fever reduction, and treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical application.
